title: Going Nuts for National Almond Day!
story: Crunch time arrives every February 16 when almond lovers everywhere celebrate National Almond Day! People munch on raw almonds, bake almond cookies, and sip almond milk to honor these tasty tree nuts. Restaurants add almond-crusted chicken to their menus, while bakeries showcase almond croissants and marzipan treats. Social media fills with almond recipe videos and fun facts about how almonds are actually seeds, not nuts!
activity_question: According to the story, what surprising fact do people share about almonds on social media?
activity_choices: Almonds are actually seeds, not nuts
Almonds are purple before they ripen
Almonds can only grow in space
activity_answer: Almonds are actually seeds, not nuts
bonus_fact: It takes about 1,000 almonds to make just one jar of almond butter!

title: Grouchy People Need Love Too!
story: Every February 16, people celebrate Do a Grouch a Favor Day by being extra kind to the grumpiest people they know! This silly holiday encourages everyone to help out cranky neighbors, moody siblings, or that one teacher who never smiles. People might bring cookies to a grouchy coworker, help an annoyed parent with chores, or simply say something nice to someone having a bad day. The idea is that even the grouchiest person might crack a smile when someone does something unexpectedly sweet for them. It's like being a kindness detective, hunting down frowns to turn upside down!
activity_question: What might people do on Do a Grouch a Favor Day?
activity_choices: Bring cookies to a grouchy coworker
Hide from cranky people all day
activity_answer: Bring cookies to a grouchy coworker
bonus_fact: Some people think this holiday was inspired by Oscar the Grouch from Sesame Street!

title: National Innovation Day: Bright Ideas Everywhere!
story: Every February 16, National Innovation Day celebrates the amazing power of human creativity and invention! People share their coolest ideas on social media, schools host invention contests, and companies showcase their latest gadgets and discoveries. Kids build robots, create apps, or design solutions to everyday problems. Museums often have special exhibits about famous inventors, while tech companies give tours and demos. Some families even have "invention challenges" at home, using cardboard and tape to build something totally new. It's a day to think outside the box and remember that every great invention started with someone saying, "What if we tried this?"
activity_question: What do some families do at home to celebrate National Innovation Day?
activity_choices: Have invention challenges using cardboard and tape
Watch movies about robots all day
activity_answer: Have invention challenges using cardboard and tape
follow_up_question: What everyday problem would you love to solve with a new invention?

title: Sweet and Chewy Gumdrop Glory Day
story: Colorful gumdrops take center stage every February 16 on National Gumdrop Day! These tiny, sugar-coated candies have been making mouths happy for over a century. People celebrate by munching on their favorite flavors, from classic orange and cherry to lime and lemon. Bakers get creative too, using gumdrops to decorate cakes, cookies, and gingerbread houses. Some families have gumdrop-sorting contests to see who can organize them by color the fastest. Candy stores often offer special deals on gumdrop varieties you might not find any other day of the year. It's the perfect excuse to indulge your sweet tooth!
activity_question: What do bakers often use gumdrops for on this holiday?
bonus_fact: The first gumdrops were made in the 1800s and were originally called "gum drops."
